### Torsion-Resistant Handed-Shearing Auxetics (HSA)-based Soft Gripper

Soft robots are ideal for delicate manipulation and safe human-robot interaction, but their lack of rigid structure limits real-world adoption. We tackled this by designing a
torsion-resistant strain limiting layer (TR-SLL) and integrating it into handed shearing auxetics to fabricate a soft gripper. The TR-SLL minimizes out-of-plane bending while 
preserving the gripper's in-plane compliance and flexibility.
The resulting TR-SLL-integrated HSA gripper demonstrated a pinch grasp payload of over 1 kg, a lifting capacity of 5 kg when loaded through the TR-SLL element. We achieved a peak pinch grasp force of 5.8 N, and a peak planar caging force of 14.5 N, a significant payload for a soft gripper lifting objects perpendicular to gravity. Read more about this work [here](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.07976){target="_blank"}.

<div style="text-align: center; margin: 20px 0;">
<video width="70%" autoplay loop muted playsinline>
  <source src="projects/HSA-Gripper-Lifting.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
<p style="font-size: 0.75rem; color: gray; margin-top: 8px;">
<em>HSA Gripper lifting objects from the YCB object dataset, to benchmark robot grasping and manipulation. The gripper achieved a success rate of 86% when tested with 43 objects.</em>
</p>
</div>

*This work was supported by the NSF, Murdock Charitable Trust, and the Office of Naval Research (ONR).*
